# UC-22.1.4 ┬Ę GDPR Data Retention Policy Enforcement

## Description

Audits Splunk index-level retention settings against written data retention policy so personal data in logs is not kept longer than necessary under the storage limitation principle.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for GDPR compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Run from a scheduled search on the search head (requires admin capability for REST); (2) replace the `case()` block with a lookup `index_retention_policy.csv` mapping index names to required max retention days; (3) export results to GRC tickets when violations trigger; (4) pair with archive/freeze path review outside Splunk for complete retention evidence.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
ŌĆó Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform (native `| rest` API, no separate TA required).
ŌĆó Ensure the following data sources are available: REST endpoint: `/services/data/indexes` ŌĆö fields: `title`, `frozenTimePeriodInSecs`, `maxTotalDataSizeMB`, `disabled`.
ŌĆó For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ŌĆö Configure data collection
(1) Run from a scheduled search on the search head (requires admin capability for REST); (2) replace the `case()` block with a lookup `index_retention_policy.csv` mapping index names to required max retention days; (3) export results to GRC tickets when violations trigger; (4) pair with archive/freeze path review outside Splunk for complete retention evidence.

Step 2 ŌĆö Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| rest /services/data/indexes splunk_server=local count=0
| search disabled=0 NOT title IN ("_*", "history", "summary")
| eval retention_days=round(frozenTimePeriodInSecs/86400, 1)
| eval policy_max_days=case(
    match(title,"^(hr|pii|gdpr)"), 180,
    match(title,"^(security|sec)"), 365,
    1=1, 365)
| eval violation=if(retention_days>policy_max_days, "Exceeds_Policy", "OK")
| table title, retention_days, policy_max_days, frozenTimePeriodInSecs, maxTotalDataSizeMB, violation
| sort - retention_days
```

Understanding this SPL

**GDPR Data Retention Policy Enforcement (Art. 5(1)(e))** ŌĆö Audits Splunk index-level retention settings against written data retention policy so personal data in logs is not kept longer than necessary under the storage limitation principle.

Documented **Data sources**: REST endpoint: `/services/data/indexes` ŌĆö fields: `title`, `frozenTimePeriodInSecs`, `maxTotalDataSizeMB`, `disabled`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform (native `| rest` API, no separate TA required). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedŌĆörename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

ŌĆó Calls Splunk `rest` to read configuration or REST-exposed entities.
ŌĆó Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
ŌĆó `eval` defines or adjusts **retention_days** ŌĆö applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
ŌĆó `eval` defines or adjusts **policy_max_days** ŌĆö applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
ŌĆó `eval` defines or adjusts **violation** ŌĆö applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

ŌĆó Orders rows with `sort` ŌĆö combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: frozenTimePeriodInSecs, maxTotalDataSizeMB, policy_max_days, retention_days, title, violation.

Step 3 ŌĆö Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 ŌĆö Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (index, retention, policy, violation), Bar chart (retention by index), Single value (violation count).

## SPL

```spl
| rest /services/data/indexes splunk_server=local count=0
| search disabled=0 NOT title IN ("_*", "history", "summary")
| eval retention_days=round(frozenTimePeriodInSecs/86400, 1)
| eval policy_max_days=case(
    match(title,"^(hr|pii|gdpr)"), 180,
    match(title,"^(security|sec)"), 365,
    1=1, 365)
| eval violation=if(retention_days>policy_max_days, "Exceeds_Policy", "OK")
| table title, retention_days, policy_max_days, frozenTimePeriodInSecs, maxTotalDataSizeMB, violation
| sort - retention_days
```

## Visualization

Table (index, retention, policy, violation), Bar chart (retention by index), Single value (violation count).
